mission.title: Install and Configure MicroK8s
mission.description: MicroK8s is a lightweight, single-package Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and speed, ideal for development, IoT, and edge computing. It provides a fully conformant Kubernetes experience with minimal overhead, making it perfect for local development environments. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to full-fledged Kubernetes clusters and reliance on the Snap package manager.

steps.title: Install MicroK8s
steps.description:
This step installs MicroK8s using Snap, which is the recommended method for installation on Linux systems.

```bash

sudo snap install microk8s --classic

```

> Installs the latest stable version of MicroK8s.

steps.title: Add User to MicroK8s Group
steps.description:
This step allows your user to run MicroK8s commands without needing sudo.

```bash

sudo usermod -a -G microk8s $USER

newgrp microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if necessary.
> Refresh your group membership.

steps.title: Enable Core Add-ons
steps.description:
This step enables essential add-ons like DNS and the Kubernetes dashboard for better functionality.

```bash

microk8s enable dns

microk8s enable dashboard

```

> Enables the DNS add-on.
> Enables the Kubernetes dashboard.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
This step checks that MicroK8s is running correctly and that the nodes are ready.

```bash

microk8s kubectl get nodes

microk8s status

```

> Should show the current node in a Ready state.
> Displays the status of MicroK8s and enabled add-ons.

uninstall.title: Uninstall MicroK8s
uninstall.description:
This step removes MicroK8s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all Kubernetes resources and configurations.

```bash

sudo snap remove microk8s

```

> This will remove MicroK8s and all associated data.

uninstall.title: Remove User from MicroK8s Group
uninstall.description:
This step removes your user from the MicroK8s group to clean up permissions.

```bash

sudo gpasswd -d $USER microk8s

```

> Replace $USER with your username if necessary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade MicroK8s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations. This step upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.

```bash

microk8s stop

sudo snap refresh microk8s

microk8s start

microk8s kubectl get nodes

```

> Stop MicroK8s before upgrading.
> Upgrades MicroK8s to the latest version.
> Start MicroK8s after the upgrade.
> Verify that the nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'microk8s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s service may not be started.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:16443: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s API server is not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'microk8s start' to start the API server.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** MicroK8s kubectl is not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Use 'microk8s kubectl' instead of 'kubectl' or add an alias.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The host machine does not meet the minimum resource requirements.

**Fix:**
Ensure your machine has at least 2 vCPU and 4GB RAM.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy add-ons that are no longer maintained.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for upgrading from v1.30.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with your node listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the resource requirements and has Snap installed.
